atoms.text: Debugging is mostly a process of elimination, not a mystery.
atoms.verbatim: it is mostly a process of elimination

atoms.text: The bug lives between the input you trust and the output you don't; the job is to keep cutting that space in half.
Guessing feels fast, but a wrong guess costs you a change, a rerun, and a little of your confidence.
Form one hypothesis you can actually test rather than spraying five changes and hoping one sticks.
The fastest debuggers read the entire error message before touching anything.
Print statements aren't shameful: a print in the right place beats an hour in a fancy debugger in the wrong place.
atoms.verbatim: a print in the right place beats an hour in a fancy debugger in the wrong place

atoms.text: When you find the bug, write the test that would have caught it, so it can never quietly come back.
Debugging is like a binary search over the space between trusted input and wrong output — halve it each step.

edges.note: writing the regression test is the smallest feedback loop
testing one hypothesis spends the focus budget well
